title: Calanthera dactyloides
taxonomy: Calanthera dactyloides is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The classification of Calanthera dactyloides is within the family Orchidaceae. This species belongs to the genus Calanthe, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Calanthera dactyloides is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae. It is also sometimes placed in the tribe Malaxideae.
uses: Calanthera dactyloides has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health issues. It is believed to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for skin infections and wounds. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses of Calanthera dactyloides.

intro: Calanthera dactyloides, commonly known as the 'bearded lip orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique, elongated flowers with a distinctive beard-like structure. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Calanthera dactyloides is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calanthera dactyloides include iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a powder or tincture to treat various ailments.
